top_signals.title: Tanker traffic through Strait of Hormuz drops to two-month low
top_signals.summary: The number of tankers moving through the Strait of Hormuz dropped on Monday to its lowest level in two months, according to shipping data, as renewed military strikes between the U.S. and Iran and attacks on vessels raised safety concerns. Oil and gas tanker traffic fell to its lowest level since May 25, based on ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: How Digitalisation Is Redefining Maritime Due Diligence
top_signals.summary: For most of shipping’s history, compliance has been a backward-looking exercise. A logbook entry record shows what has already happened. An audit checks what was already done. A vetting inspection samples a single day in a vessel’s life and extrapolates a verdict from it. Due diligence, in this model, is really an act of reconstruction: ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Iranian floating crude storage decreases in June as sanctions relief clears cargoes
top_signals.summary: Floating storage of crude and condensate in East Asian waters declined month over month in June, with Southeast Asia volumes returning to the year-ago level as the Iranian crude overhang, which had built up since late 2025, unwound. The fall came as the US has suspended its blockage on seaborne Iranian trades since mid-June and ...

top_signals.title: ETF IQ 7/13/2026
top_signals.summary: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests:  Former AQR Capital Management Head of Financial Market Research &  Author of "Wrong Number" Aaron Brown, Capital Management CEO & CIO Matthew Tuttle,  JPMorgan Asset Management Global Head of ETFs Travis Spence, and Smead Capital CEO Cole Smead.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: People as Proof: why Greek shipping still needs human judgement behind vessel data
top_signals.summary: Greek shipping has always relied on judgement. Data matters, but owners, brokers, buyers and financiers have never made decisions on numbers alone. They look at the vessel, the timing, the market, the counterparty and the people behind the information. That is why the current debate around technology in shipping needs more balance. The industry now ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.summary: For the most part, China prohibits crypto trading and services while authorities actively investigate and prosecute crimes.

top_signals.title: Chinese gas stocks rally after Beijing halts helium exports
top_signals.summary: Chinese natural and industrial gas stocks rallied on Monday after Beijing imposed a temporary ban on helium exports, citing the strategic importance of industrial gases used in semiconductor manufacturing. Industrial gas makers led the gains, with Shanghai-listed Suzhou Jinhong Gas (SS:688106) shares surging nearly 10%. Guangzhou Guanggang Gases & Energy (G-Gas) (SS:688548) shares climbed as ...

top_signals.title: Afore Banamex Chief Investment Officer Acosta to Leave Firm
top_signals.summary: The chief investment officer of Mexican pension fund Afore Banamex, Tomas Acosta, is leaving the firm, according to people familiar with the matter.

top_signals.title: EU leaders eye social media ban for children under age 13
top_signals.summary: “While ultimately it is up to parents to decide when children get their first smartphones, what we already have is a consensus that there needs to be a start date for the age children can join social media,” says European Commission President Ursula van der Leyen.

Baltic Dry Index Rises for 3rd Day
alerts.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, advanced for the third session on Monday, rising about 0.5% to a fresh high since June 5 at 2,960 points. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and coal, also increased for the third day, ...
